summary: Moved most of the DamageSong common-fighter action code out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s` into a new `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageSong.c` and header, reaching a reported 97.90% file match. The PR added C implementations for DamageSong, DamageSongWait, DamageSongRv anim/IASA/phys/coll callbacks plus helpers around motion-state transitions and a boolean guard, expanded `ftCommonData` offsets 0x624-0x644 into float fields used by this code, and wired the new C object and includes into the build.

key_files.role: new C translation unit replacing most of the DamageSong assembly slice
new public header declaring DamageSong callbacks and `ftCo_800C3538`
removed the matched DamageSong assembly and sdata2 literals while leaving later ft_0C31 assembly
removed DamageSong declarations from the generic ft_0C31 header and changed the include guard to `GALE01_0C31`
expanded `struct ftCommonData` offsets 0x624 through 0x644 into named float slots used by DamageSong formulas

what_changed: Added `ftCo_DamageSong.c` with C or gated ASM implementations for `ftCo_800C318C`, `ftCo_DamageSong_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageSong_IASA`, `ftCo_DamageSong_Phys`, `ftCo_DamageSong_Coll`, `ftCo_800C3390`, `ftCo_DamageSongWait_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageSongWait_IASA`, `ftCo_DamageSongWait_Phys`, `ftCo_DamageSongWait_Coll`, `ftCo_800C3480`, `ftCo_DamageSongRv_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageSongRv_IASA`, `ftCo_DamageSongRv_Phys`, `ftCo_DamageSongRv_Coll`, and `ftCo_800C3538`.
Removed the corresponding assembly block from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s`, including the local sdata2 literals `ftCo_804D8CC0`, `ftCo_804D8CC8`, and `ftCo_804D8CCC`.
Declared DamageSong symbols in a dedicated `ftCo_DamageSong.h` and removed them from `ft_0C31.h`, leaving `ftCo_800C3598` and later ft_0C31 declarations in the old header.
Expanded `struct ftCommonData` from an opaque byte range at `+0x624` into floats `x624`, `x628`, `x62C`, `x630`, `x634`, `x638`, `x63C`, `x640`, and `x644`, followed by padding from `+0x648`.
Added the new source object to `TEXT_O_FILES` before the residual `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s.o` entry.
The PR report says all listed functions matched 100% except `ftCo_800C318C`, which scored 92.43%, producing a 97.90% file match.
smart_moves: Split the newly decompiled action code into `ftCo_DamageSong.c`/`.h` instead of continuing to expose DamageSong declarations from the broad `ft_0C31.h`; this improves locality while preserving the remaining ft_0C31 assembly boundary.

Kept small callback wrappers simple and idiomatic: IASA callbacks are empty, phys callbacks call `ft_80084F3C`, coll callbacks call `ft_80084280`, and reverse anim returns to `ft_8008A2BC` when `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` is false.

Factored repeated timer/decrement behavior into `inlineB0`, shared by `ftCo_DamageSong_Anim` and `ftCo_DamageSongWait_Anim`, matching repeated assembly shape while reducing duplication in the C source.

Factored the entry knockback/damage song calculation into `inlineA0`, explicitly documenting the dependency on `fp->dmg.x1830_percent`, player handicap, `Player_80033BB8`, and `p_ftCommonData` floats.
Used static declarations for internal transition helpers `ftCo_800C3390` and `ftCo_800C3480`, avoiding unnecessary header surface for functions only used inside the new translation unit.
Used `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` C bodies with inline assembly fallbacks for difficult functions, preserving matching infrastructure where exact C was not yet sufficient.
decomp_lessons: When extracting a subsection from a larger asm file, add the new C object before the remaining asm object in `obj_files.mk` so symbol order stays compatible with the existing split.
Move action-specific declarations out of address-range headers once a coherent `ftCo_*` action file exists; keep the range header for symbols that still belong to the residual asm slice.

Opaque common-data padding can often be safely narrowed by reading the decompiled formula: this PR identified consecutive float fields at `ftCommonData` offsets `0x624` through `0x644` because the DamageSong formula loads each as `lfs`.

For repeated assembly patterns, introduce `static inline` helpers only when their use reproduces the call/branch shape; here `inlineB0` captures the common countdown-and-transition behavior used by both DamageSong and DamageSongWait anim callbacks.
Simple fighter action callbacks are good candidates for direct C replacement: empty IASA functions, phys/coll passthroughs, and frame-remaining checks tend to match cleanly.

Keep evidence of non-perfect matches in the record: despite many 100% functions, `ftCo_800C318C` remained at 92.43%, so future work should revisit that entry function rather than assuming the whole file is complete.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_DamageSong_*`, `ftCo_DamageSongWait_*`, and `ftCo_DamageSongRv_*` follow the common fighter action callback suffixes `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
Unknown address-based helpers retained `ftCo_800Cxxxx` names, e.g. `ftCo_800C318C`, `ftCo_800C3390`, `ftCo_800C3480`, `ftCo_800C3538`, and existing `ftCo_800C3598`.
The new header uses an address-derived include guard `GALE01_0C318C`; `ft_0C31.h` was adjusted to `GALE01_0C31` after moving the `0C318C` declarations out.
Newly exposed `ftCommonData` fields used offset-style placeholder names `x624` through `x644`, consistent with partially understood struct layout work.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted removed sdata2 literals from assembly into C constants: `double const ftCo_804D8CC0 = S32_TO_F32`, `float const ftCo_804D8CC8 = 0`, and `float const ftCo_804D8CCC = 1`.

Used `ASM void` and `nofralloc` inline assembly blocks under the `MUST_MATCH` path for hard-to-match functions such as `ftCo_800C318C`, `ftCo_DamageSong_Anim`, `ftCo_800C3390`, `ftCo_DamageSongWait_Anim`, and `ftCo_800C3480`.
Recreated motion-state calls with named enums and flags: `ftCo_MS_DamageSong`, `ftCo_MS_DamageSongWait`, `ftCo_MS_DamageSongRv`, `Ft_MF_None`, and `Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim | Ft_MF_SkipColAnim`.
Matched integer-to-float conversion behavior through the shared `S32_TO_F32` literal and formula use of `Player_GetHandicap` and `Player_80033BB8`.

Preserved branch behavior in `ftCo_800C3538` by translating bit/field checks into `fp->x2222_b2` and a `switch` over `fp->x2070.x2071_b0_3` that rejects cases 9, 10, and 11 before calling `ftCo_800C3598`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR dump.
searchable_terms: ftCommonData x624 x628 x62C x630 x634 x638 x63C x640 x644
follow_up_queries: Revisit `ftCo_800C318C` to close the remaining 92.43% match gap and determine whether the C `inlineA0` formula can be reordered or typed to match without inline ASM.
Search later PRs for renames of `ftCommonData` fields `x624` through `x644` to semantic names based on DamageSong behavior.
Check whether `inlineA0` and `inlineB0` survived later cleanup or were replaced by named helpers once more common fighter damage code was understood.
Look for follow-up PRs that remove more symbols from `asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s` after this split.
